H01.question: Which late suppliers are hurting our cash flow the most, and through which SKUs?
H01.sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (SELECT DISTINCT item_key, supplier_key FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line),
        sup AS (SELECT s.supplier_key, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, AVG(po.late_days) late
                FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key) GROUP BY 1,2,3),
        rev AS (SELECT m.supplier_key, SUM(sol.ext_amount) affected_revenue
                FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key) GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT sup.supplier_name, sup.reliability_tier, ROUND(sup.late,1) AS avg_late_days,
               ROUND(rev.affected_revenue,2) AS affected_revenue
        FROM sup JOIN rev USING (supplier_key)
        WHERE sup.late > 5 ORDER BY rev.affected_revenue DESC LIMIT 5
H01.sanity_note: top suppliers must be tier C/D

H02.question: Explain whether our CCC problem is driven more by slow customers, excess inventory, or supplier payment timing.
H02.sql:
SELECT 'DSO' AS component, ROUND((SELECT SUM(days_to_pay*amount_applied)/SUM(amount_applied) FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application),2) AS days
        UNION ALL
        SELECT 'DIO', ROUND((SELECT (SELECT AVG(dv) FROM (SELECT snapshot_date_key, SUM(value_on_hand) dv FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1))
                             / (SELECT SUM(ext_cost)/COUNT(DISTINCT order_date_key) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line)),2)
        UNION ALL
        SELECT 'DPO', ROUND((SELECT SUM(days_to_pay*amount_paid)/SUM(amount_paid) FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment),2)
H02.sanity_note: DIO must be the largest component

H03.question: Which suppliers are late but still being paid faster than average?
H03.sql:
WITH sup_late AS (SELECT supplier_key, AVG(late_days) late FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line GROUP BY 1),
        sup_pay AS (SELECT supplier_key, SUM(days_to_pay*amount_paid)/SUM(amount_paid) pay FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, ROUND(l.late,1) AS avg_late_days, ROUND(p.pay,1) AS days_to_pay
        FROM sup_late l JOIN sup_pay p USING (supplier_key) JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        WHERE l.late > (SELECT AVG(late_days) FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line)
          AND p.pay < (SELECT SUM(days_to_pay*amount_paid)/SUM(amount_paid) FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment)
        ORDER BY l.late DESC
H03.sanity_note: listed suppliers are late (above-avg) yet paid faster than the 39d weighted average

H04.question: Which item categories create the worst combination of high DIO and high stockout rate?
H04.sql:
WITH cat_inv AS (
          SELECT i.category, AVG(dv) avg_inv, AVG(so_pct) stockout_pct FROM (
            SELECT snap.item_key, snap.snapshot_date_key, SUM(snap.value_on_hand) dv,
                   AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) so_pct
            FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap GROUP BY 1,2) x
          JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key) GROUP BY 1),
        cat_daily AS (
          SELECT i.category, SUM(dv)/COUNT(DISTINCT snapshot_date_key) daily_inv
          FROM (SELECT item_key, snapshot_date_key, SUM(value_on_hand) dv FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1,2) x
          JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key) GROUP BY 1),
        cat_cogs AS (
          SELECT i.category, SUM(sol.ext_cost)/COUNT(DISTINCT sol.order_date_key) daily_cogs
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key) GROUP BY 1),
        cat_so AS (
          SELECT i.category, AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) stockout_pct
          FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key) GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT d.category, ROUND(d.daily_inv/c.daily_cogs,1) AS dio_days, ROUND(so.stockout_pct,2) AS stockout_pct
        FROM cat_daily d JOIN cat_cogs c USING (category) JOIN cat_so so USING (category)
        ORDER BY (d.daily_inv/c.daily_cogs) * so.stockout_pct DESC
H04.sanity_note: ranking driven by stockout x dio product

H05.question: Which customer segments generate high revenue but worsen cash conversion due to delayed payments?
H05.sql:
WITH seg_rev AS (
          SELECT c.segment, SUM(sol.ext_amount) revenue
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key) GROUP BY 1),
        seg_pay AS (
          SELECT c.segment, SUM(pa.days_to_pay*pa.amount_applied)/SUM(pa.amount_applied) dtp
          FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key) GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT r.segment, ROUND(r.revenue,2) AS revenue, ROUND(p.dtp,1) AS collection_days
        FROM seg_rev r JOIN seg_pay p USING (segment) ORDER BY r.revenue DESC

H06.question: Which subsidiaries have the highest CCC, and which component is responsible: DSO, DIO, or DPO?
H06.sql:
WITH sub_dso AS (
          SELECT subsidiary_key, SUM(days_to_pay*amount_applied)/SUM(amount_applied) dso
          FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application GROUP BY 1),
        sub_dpo AS (
          SELECT subsidiary_key, SUM(days_to_pay*amount_paid)/SUM(amount_paid) dpo
          FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment GROUP BY 1),
        sub_inv AS (
          SELECT subsidiary_key, SUM(dv)/COUNT(DISTINCT snapshot_date_key) daily_inv FROM
            (SELECT subsidiary_key, snapshot_date_key, SUM(value_on_hand) dv FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1,2) x
          GROUP BY 1),
        sub_cogs AS (
          SELECT subsidiary_key, SUM(ext_cost)/COUNT(DISTINCT order_date_key) daily_cogs
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT sub.subsidiary_name,
               ROUND(dso.dso,1) AS dso_days,
               ROUND(COALESCE(inv.daily_inv/cogs.daily_cogs, 0),1) AS dio_days,
               ROUND(dpo.dpo,1) AS dpo_days,
               ROUND(dso.dso + COALESCE(inv.daily_inv/cogs.daily_cogs, 0) - dpo.dpo,1) AS ccc_days
        FROM mro_distributor.dim_subsidiary sub
        JOIN sub_dso dso USING (subsidiary_key)
        JOIN sub_dpo dpo USING (subsidiary_key)
        JOIN sub_cogs cogs USING (subsidiary_key)
        LEFT JOIN sub_inv inv USING (subsidiary_key)
        ORDER BY ccc_days DESC
H06.sanity_note: subsidiary 1 carries all inventory (data quirk) so its CCC dominates via DIO

H07.question: Which suppliers indirectly delay customer cash collection through late receipts and delayed fulfillment?
H07.sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (SELECT DISTINCT item_key, supplier_key FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line),
        sup_late AS (SELECT supplier_key, AVG(late_days) late FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line GROUP BY 1),
        sup_ots AS (
          SELECT m.supplier_key, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) ots
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key) GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, ROUND(l.late,1) AS avg_late_days, ROUND(o.ots,1) AS avg_order_to_ship_days
        FROM sup_late l JOIN sup_ots o USING (supplier_key) JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        WHERE l.late > 5 ORDER BY o.ots DESC LIMIT 10
H07.sanity_note: top suppliers must be tier C/D with elevated order-to-ship

H08.question: Show the top supplier-item pairs where late procurement caused stockouts and poor OTIF.
H08.sql:
WITH pairs AS (SELECT supplier_key, item_key, AVG(late_days) late FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line GROUP BY 1,2),
        item_so AS (SELECT item_key, AVG(CASE WHEN is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) so_pct FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1),
        item_otif AS (
          SELECT sol.item_key,
                 COUNT(CASE WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NOT NULL AND ff.ship_date_key <= sol.promised_ship_date_key
                             AND ff.qty_shipped >= sol.qty_ordered THEN 1 END)*100.0/COUNT(*) otif
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key) GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, i.sku, ROUND(p.late,1) AS avg_late_days,
               ROUND(so.so_pct,1) AS stockout_pct, ROUND(ot.otif,1) AS otif_pct
        FROM pairs p
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key)
        JOIN item_so so USING (item_key)
        JOIN item_otif ot USING (item_key)
        WHERE p.late > 5 AND ot.otif < 50
        ORDER BY so.so_pct DESC, s.supplier_key, i.sku LIMIT 10
H08.sanity_note: top pairs must be tier C/D sourced
H08.note: D-038: added ORDER BY tiebreaker (supplier_key, sku) — original query had unstable row order among tied so_pct values, unrelated to the name patch.

H09.question: If we replaced our tier-D suppliers, how much order-to-ship delay would we eliminate on affected SKUs?
H09.sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (SELECT DISTINCT item_key, supplier_key FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line),
        tier_ots AS (
          SELECT s.reliability_tier, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) ots, COUNT(*) ff_lines
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff
          JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
          JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
          GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT ROUND(d.ots - a.ots, 2) AS delay_days_eliminated_per_line, d.ff_lines AS affected_shipments
        FROM (SELECT * FROM tier_ots WHERE reliability_tier='D') d,
             (SELECT * FROM tier_ots WHERE reliability_tier='A') a
H09.sanity_note: delta ~5 days (D ots ~7.0 vs A ots ~2.0)

H10.question: Which items should we dual-source first, given supplier lateness and their stockout/revenue impact?
H10.sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (SELECT DISTINCT item_key, supplier_key FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line),
        item_late AS (SELECT item_key, AVG(late_days) late FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line GROUP BY 1),
        item_rev AS (SELECT item_key, SUM(ext_amount) revenue FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line GROUP BY 1),
        item_so AS (SELECT item_key, AVG(CASE WHEN is_stockout THEN 1.0 ELSE 0 END) so_rate FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT i.sku, s.supplier_name, s.reliability_tier, ROUND(l.late,1) AS avg_late_days,
               ROUND(r.revenue,2) AS revenue, ROUND(so.so_rate*100,1) AS stockout_pct
        FROM item_late l
        JOIN item_rev r USING (item_key)
        JOIN item_so so USING (item_key)
        JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key)
        ORDER BY l.late * r.revenue * so.so_rate DESC LIMIT 10
H10.sanity_note: top items must be tier C/D sourced

H11.question: Compare fast-moving versus slow-moving items: which group suffers more from supplier lateness?
H11.sql:
WITH vel AS (SELECT item_key, SUM(qty_shipped) units FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line GROUP BY 1),
        cut AS (SELECT quantile_cont(units,0.8) hi, quantile_cont(units,0.2) lo FROM vel),
        cls AS (SELECT v.item_key, CASE WHEN v.units >= cut.hi THEN 'fast' WHEN v.units <= cut.lo THEN 'slow' END grp FROM vel v, cut),
        item_supplier AS (SELECT DISTINCT item_key, supplier_key FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line),
        grp_late AS (
          SELECT c.grp, AVG(po.late_days) late
          FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po JOIN cls c USING (item_key) WHERE c.grp IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1),
        grp_ots AS (
          SELECT c.grp, AVG(ff.order_to_ship_days) ots
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff JOIN cls c USING (item_key) WHERE c.grp IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1),
        grp_so AS (
          SELECT c.grp, AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END) so_pct
          FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap JOIN cls c USING (item_key) WHERE c.grp IS NOT NULL GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT l.grp AS mover_group, ROUND(l.late,2) AS avg_supplier_late_days,
               ROUND(o.ots,2) AS avg_order_to_ship_days, ROUND(so.so_pct,2) AS stockout_pct
        FROM grp_late l JOIN grp_ots o USING (grp) JOIN grp_so so USING (grp) ORDER BY 1
H11.sanity_note: expected finding: both groups suffer similarly (lateness is supplier-driven, not velocity-driven)

H12.question: Did Q4 revenue seasonality worsen stockouts, and which suppliers failed to keep up with the demand spike?
H12.sql:
SELECT CASE WHEN d.month_num IN (10,11,12) THEN 'Q4' ELSE 'non-Q4' END AS period,
               ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN snap.is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END),2) AS stockout_pct
        FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap JOIN mro_distributor.dim_date d ON snap.snapshot_date_key = d.date_key
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
H12.sanity_note: expected finding: stockouts are FLAT Q4 vs non-Q4 (generator does not demand-couple stockouts) — the correct answer is 'no material worsening'

H13.question: Which customers are hit twice: buying items from unreliable suppliers and experiencing our slowest fulfillment?
H13.sql:
WITH item_supplier AS (SELECT DISTINCT item_key, supplier_key FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line),
        cust_cd AS (
          SELECT sol.customer_key,
                 SUM(CASE WHEN s.reliability_tier IN ('C','D') THEN sol.ext_amount ELSE 0 END)*100.0/SUM(sol.ext_amount) cd_pct,
                 SUM(sol.ext_amount) revenue
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
          JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key)
          JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
          GROUP BY 1),
        cust_ots AS (SELECT customer_key, AVG(order_to_ship_days) ots FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line GROUP BY 1)
        SELECT c.customer_name, ROUND(cd.cd_pct,1) AS pct_revenue_from_cd_suppliers,
               ROUND(o.ots,2) AS avg_fulfillment_days, ROUND(cd.revenue,2) AS revenue
        FROM cust_cd cd
        JOIN cust_ots o USING (customer_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key)
        WHERE cd.cd_pct > (SELECT SUM(CASE WHEN s.reliability_tier IN ('C','D') THEN sol.ext_amount ELSE 0 END)*100.0/SUM(sol.ext_amount)
                           FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN item_supplier m USING (item_key) JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key))
          AND o.ots > (SELECT AVG(order_to_ship_days) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line)
        ORDER BY cd.revenue DESC LIMIT 10
H13.sanity_note: listed customers have above-average C/D revenue share AND above-average fulfillment days

H14.question: Break down our OTIF misses: what share is late-only, short-only, or both, and which suppliers sit behind the late portion?
H14.sql:
WITH j AS (
          SELECT sol.so_line_key, sol.qty_ordered, sol.promised_ship_date_key, ff.ship_date_key, ff.qty_shipped
          FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)),
        cat AS (
          SELECT CASE
            WHEN ship_date_key IS NULL THEN 'never_shipped'
            WHEN ship_date_key > promised_ship_date_key AND qty_shipped >= qty_ordered THEN 'late_only'
            WHEN ship_date_key <= promised_ship_date_key AND qty_shipped < qty_ordered THEN 'short_only'
            WHEN ship_date_key > promised_ship_date_key AND qty_shipped < qty_ordered THEN 'late_and_short'
            ELSE 'hit' END miss_type
          FROM j)
        SELECT miss_type, COUNT(*) AS lines,
               ROUND(COUNT(*)*100.0/(SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cat WHERE miss_type != 'hit'),1) AS pct_of_misses
        FROM cat WHERE miss_type != 'hit' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC
H14.sanity_note: short_only and late_and_short must be 0% (generator ships full quantities) — honest data reality

M01.question: Show revenue by month. Is there seasonality?
M01.sql:
SELECT d.year_num, d.month_num, ROUND(SUM(sol.ext_amount),2) AS revenue
        FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN mro_distributor.dim_date d ON sol.order_date_key = d.date_key
        GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,2

M02.question: Which item category has the best gross margin, and which has the worst?
M02.sql:
SELECT i.category, ROUND(SUM(sol.margin_amount)/SUM(sol.ext_amount)*100,2) AS gm_pct
        FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN mro_distributor.dim_item i USING (item_key)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

M03.question: Rank suppliers by average PO late days. Who are the worst five?
M03.sql:
SELECT s.supplier_name, ROUND(AVG(po.late_days),2) AS avg_late_days
        FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 5

M04.question: What is on-time delivery rate by supplier reliability tier?
M04.sql:
SELECT s.reliability_tier, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN po.late_days = 0 THEN 1 END)*100.0/COUNT(*),2) AS otd_pct
        FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

M05.question: Show revenue by customer segment.
M05.sql:
SELECT c.segment, ROUND(SUM(sol.ext_amount),2) AS revenue
        FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

M06.question: Which warehouse holds the most inventory value?
M06.sql:
SELECT w.warehouse_name, ROUND(SUM(snap.value_on_hand),2) AS inv_value
        FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap JOIN mro_distributor.dim_warehouse w USING (warehouse_key)
        WHERE snap.snapshot_date_key = (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date_key) FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

M07.question: What share of invoices is overdue by customer segment?
M07.sql:
SELECT c.segment, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN pa.payment_date_key > i.due_date_key THEN 1 END)*100.0/COUNT(*),2) AS overdue_pct
        FROM mro_distributor.ar_invoice i
        JOIN mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa USING (invoice_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c ON i.customer_key = c.customer_key
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

M08.question: Who are our top 10 suppliers by spend?
M08.sql:
SELECT s.supplier_name, ROUND(SUM(po.ext_cost),2) AS spend
        FROM mro_distributor.p2p_purchase_order_line po JOIN mro_distributor.dim_supplier s USING (supplier_key)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC LIMIT 10

M09.question: How has monthly COGS trended over the last 12 months?
M09.sql:
SELECT d.year_num, d.month_num, ROUND(SUM(b.period_balance),2) AS gl_cogs
        FROM mro_distributor.gl_account_balance b
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_gl_account a USING (gl_account_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_date d ON b.period_date_key = d.date_key
        WHERE a.account_name = 'COGS' AND d.year_num = 2025
        GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,2

M10.question: Which items are slow movers, and what is their tied-up inventory value?
M10.sql:
WITH velocity AS (SELECT item_key, SUM(qty_shipped) units FROM mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line GROUP BY 1),
        cutoff AS (SELECT quantile_cont(units, 0.2) q FROM velocity)
        SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT snap.item_key) AS slow_mover_items, ROUND(SUM(snap.value_on_hand),2) AS tied_up_value
        FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot snap JOIN velocity v USING (item_key), cutoff
        WHERE snap.snapshot_date_key = (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date_key) FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot)
          AND v.units <= cutoff.q

M11.question: What is average days-to-pay versus credit terms by customer segment?
M11.sql:
SELECT c.segment, ROUND(AVG(pa.days_to_pay - c.credit_days),2) AS days_beyond_terms
        FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application pa JOIN mro_distributor.dim_customer c USING (customer_key)
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2 DESC

M12.question: What is our OTIF rate by warehouse?
M12.sql:
SELECT w.warehouse_name,
               ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN ff.ship_date_key IS NOT NULL AND ff.ship_date_key <= sol.promised_ship_date_key
                                 AND ff.qty_shipped >= sol.qty_ordered THEN 1 END)*100.0/COUNT(*),2) AS otif_pct
        FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line sol
        LEFT JOIN mro_distributor.o2c_fulfillment_line ff USING (so_line_key)
        JOIN mro_distributor.dim_warehouse w ON sol.warehouse_key = w.warehouse_key
        GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 2

S04.question: What is our current DIO?
S04.sql:
SELECT ROUND(
          (SELECT AVG(dv) FROM (SELECT snapshot_date_key, SUM(value_on_hand) dv FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1))
          / (SELECT SUM(ext_cost)/COUNT(DISTINCT order_date_key) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line), 2)
columns: round(((SELECT avg(dv) FROM (SELECT snapshot_date_key, sum(value_on_hand) AS dv FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1)) / (SELECT (sum(ext_cost) / count(DISTINCT order_date_key)) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line)), 2)

S05.question: What is our cash conversion cycle?
S05.sql:
SELECT ROUND(dso.v + dio.v - dpo.v, 2) FROM
          (SELECT SUM(days_to_pay*amount_applied)/SUM(amount_applied) v FROM mro_distributor.ar_payment_application) dso,
          (SELECT (SELECT AVG(dv) FROM (SELECT snapshot_date_key, SUM(value_on_hand) dv FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot GROUP BY 1))
                  / (SELECT SUM(ext_cost)/COUNT(DISTINCT order_date_key) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line) v) dio,
          (SELECT SUM(days_to_pay*amount_paid)/SUM(amount_paid) v FROM mro_distributor.p2p_bill_payment) dpo

S06.question: What is our overall gross margin percentage?

S07.question: What is our current stockout rate?
S07.sql: SELECT ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN is_stockout THEN 100.0 ELSE 0 END),2) FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot

S08.question: What is the total value of inventory on hand?
S08.sql:
SELECT ROUND(SUM(value_on_hand),2) FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot
        WHERE snapshot_date_key = (SELECT MAX(snapshot_date_key) FROM mro_distributor.inv_balance_snapshot)

S09.question: What is our average order value?
S09.sql:
SELECT ROUND(AVG(order_total),2) FROM (
          SELECT so_number, SUM(ext_amount) AS order_total FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line GROUP BY so_number)

S10.question: What is our return rate?
S10.sql:
SELECT ROUND((SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_return_line) * 100.0
                     / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line), 2)
columns: round((((SELECT count_star() FROM mro_distributor.o2c_return_line) * 100.0) / (SELECT count_star() FROM mro_distributor.o2c_sales_order_line)), 2)
